notes: Exceptional work across all dimensions. Criterion 1: Clearly explains both z-score (|z|>2) and IQR methods with exact thresholds (mean=67.01, std=12.03, Q1=57.16, Q3=76.41), reports all 6 global z-outliers with specific z-scores, correctly identifies that IQR found no outliers and explains why. Criterion 2: Comprehensively covers all three anomaly types—6 global outliers, 6 within-continent outliers (with z-scores ranging from -3.43 to +2.27), 102 temporal declines identified, top 5 single-period drops ranked with magnitudes. Criterion 3: Connects outliers to real historical events—HIV/AIDS (southern Africa 1990–2005, >20% prevalence mentioned), Rwandan genocide (1994), Khmer Rouge (1975–1979), Great Leap Forward (1959–1961, 15–45 million deaths), Somali/Liberian civil wars, Gulf War/Iraq sanctions, Afghan wars, North Korean famine. Criterion 4: Professional structure with 6 logical sections, multiple formatted tables, 4 referenced charts, supporting Python analysis scripts (analyze.py, charts.py), JSON results, and clear progression from methodology through findings to interpretation. All expected values match: Rwanda 1987→1992 -20.42 years, Afghanistan z=-3.43, six z-outliers correctly identified.
